name: ground_truth_au5_audit_alert_policy_compliant
description: Ground-truth fixture from active Example System IATT CCIS workbook (row 80, CCI-001572, assessed 2026-05-29). Compliant verdict based on audit-processing-failure alerting SOP, SIEM configuration, and monitoring dashboards. Narrative uses structured headings (Policy/Implementation/Monitoring) with 'Examined' and 'Confirmed' affirming phrases. Evidence type: SOP + SIEM configuration + operational procedures. Tests multi-paragraph structured narratives.

ccis_row.guidance: 001572: The organization defines the personnel or roles to be alerted in the event of an audit processing failure.
ccis_row.procedures: The organization conducting the inspection/assessment obtains and examines the documented list of personnel or roles who should be alerted in the event of audit processing failure to ensure the organization being inspected/assessed has either defined additional personnel or roles, or identified that there are no additional personnel or roles.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- Auditing Procedures SOP (SharePoint: Cybersecurity/Account Management Artifacts/Procedures) — defines audit-processing-failure conditions and notification procedures.
- SIEM configuration export — confirms audit data ingestion from all boundary components with critical alerting enabled.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative:
Policy:
Examined Auditing Procedures SOP; audit-processing-failure conditions (log-flow stoppage, ingest backlog) are defined and documented.

Implementation:
Confirmed SIEM ingests audit data from all boundary components. Critical alerts are surfaced through monitoring infrastructure.

Monitoring:
Security operators conduct daily dashboard reviews; alerts are documented and addressed per the SOP, with weekly reports retained for authorization. Audit-processing-failure conditions are visible to operators via the same daily dashboard review path.
